Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jan-09-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff James Bradford Jr.
Constance Fraenkel of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel.

RULING: NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) and (d). The trial date is April 29, 2019 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is May 9, 2019. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is April 25, 2019. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P. unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is April 12, 2019. Expert discovery cut-off date is April 26, 2019. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than February 8, 2019. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini minutes continued, see next entry)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Jan-09-2019 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than January 23, 2019 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than January 23, 2019 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on March 13, 2019 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to taking the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than March 15, 2019. Responses to the demand are due no later than March 29, 2019. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by April 19, 2019 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, April 25, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File An Answer In Intervention
Off Calendar Dec-13-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James C. Parker of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing for non parties National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburgh, PA and TIG Insurance Company.

RULING: National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburg PA and TIG Insurance Company's Motion for Order Granting to Leave to File An Answer in Intervention

No opposition and tentative ruling adopted: National Union Fire Insurance Company of Pittsburg PA and TIG Insurance Company's Motion for Order Granting to Leave to File An Answer in Intervention is GRANTED. Plaintiffs offer no authority for the proposition that intervenors are required, at this time, to provide the requested disclosures. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
